an give an indication of the sex of the native. According to
one ancient theory the first and third decanates of an odd sign
are male, and those for an even sign are female. But this
cannot hold good. We have to consider the sign and its ruler.
This may determine the sex. The drekkana or the navamsa of
the Moon and lagna at the time of ovulation may help us
determine the sex. But the time of ovulation cannot be known.
This is a wild goose chase.
Let us return to the theory of rectification of the time of
birth on the basis ofthe Pre-natal Epoch. The procedure is as
follows:
i. From the given time, place, and date of birth ascertain
the degrees of the lagna and ofthe Moon for the latitude
and longitude of the place.
ii. If the Moon is below the horizon, note its distance from
the lagna; and if the Moon is above the horizon, note its
distance from the cusp of the seventh house. Divide this
product by thirteen. The result is the number of days.
Call it "A" days.
